title: Laws as Recipes
focus: Framed alcohol statutes as combinations of price, access, and enforcement mechanisms rather than simple yes/no policy checkboxes.

title: Panel Assembly
focus: Merged APIS, FARS, FHWA, FRED, and YRBS into a state-year research panel with common state and year keys.

title: Rates and Exposure
focus: Moved beyond crash counts by using population-normalized and exposure-aware outcomes.

title: Teen Data Humility
focus: Identified sparse YRBS coverage, making teen drinking outcomes secondary rather than the primary evidence base.

title: Text Becomes Signal
focus: Turned policy language into mechanism features that forecasting models can use.

title: Mechanism Split
focus: Separated law text into price, access, and enforcement channels so policy changes can be interpreted.

title: Forecasting Value
focus: Showed that mechanism-aware text adds measurable signal compared with policy-only baselines.

title: Enforcement Pattern
focus: Found enforcement language was often the strongest interpretive signal, while avoiding causal overclaiming.

title: Model Ladder
focus: Compared forecasting models and selected Random Forest for next-year impaired-fatality prediction.

title: Equations and Limits
focus: Explained RMSE, MAE, R-squared, and why predictive accuracy is not the same as causal proof.

title: Final Product Build
focus: Turned the research package into a public, teacher-facing dashboard with simulations and transparent uncertainty.
